summary: Vanessa Cruz, VP of Engineering at Coastal Living Co, contacted Aegis Cloud Security support regarding a critical MFA token failure affecting approximately 40-45 users since 7 AM. The issue involves TOTP validation failures (error 4031) for users authenticating through an Okta SSO integration with Aegis Identity. Support agent Elena Vasquez determined the issue is likely on Aegis's end after ruling out client-side clock drift, and escalated it as a P1 business-critical incident. A temporary workaround using email OTP was implemented to unblock the engineering and ops teams while the root cause is investigated.
actionItems: Elena Vasquez: Escalate case 3677 as P1 business-critical to Identity engineering team with active user lockout flag
Elena Vasquez: Send follow-up email to Vanessa with root cause update by end of business today
Elena Vasquez: Notify Vanessa's account team to schedule a broader reliability discussion
Vanessa Cruz: Enable email OTP fallback in Aegis Identity admin console and notify affected users to retry login

keyMoments.text: Vanessa reveals 40-45 users are locked out of production systems, emphasizing the critical business impact during spring inventory cycle
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz

keyMoments.text: Elena acknowledges the issue appears to be on Aegis's end based on 4031 error logs and failed re-enrollment attempts
keyMoments.speaker: Elena Vasquez

keyMoments.text: Elena offers email OTP as a temporary fallback workaround and commits to a P1 escalation with 2-4 hour resolution path
keyMoments.speaker: Elena Vasquez

keyMoments.text: Vanessa states this is not the first reliability issue and signals intent to have a broader conversation with the account team
keyMoments.speaker: Vanessa Cruz
